item.title: El Niño Threatens to Deepen Crisis for Brazilian Rice Farmers
item.description: A looming El Niño is threatening to deepen a financial crisis for rice farmers in southern Brazil, where years of extreme weather have already sent costs soaring and forced the country’s top producing region to slash future planting plans.

item.title: China Births Two Robot Unicorns as Sector Funding Stays Strong
item.description: Two Chinese robotics startups have been valued at more than $2.9 billion in recent funding rounds, demonstrating steady interest in the sector and boosting the country’s efforts to compete with US firms like Tesla Inc. and Figure AI Inc.

item.title: JPMorgan’s Matejka Is Biggest Europe Stocks Bull With New Target
item.description: As European stocks were hammered by the Iran war, JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s Mislav Matejka predicted the declines would be short-lived. The strategist is now doubling down, becoming the biggest bull among forecasters tracked by Bloomberg.

item.title: AirTrunk Is Said to Be Near Confidential Filing for Biggest Singapore IPO in Years
item.description: Blackstone Inc.-backed data center operator AirTrunk is close to filing confidentially for an initial public offering of a real estate investment trust in Singapore, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Chinese Investors Review Bond Holdings as Ratings Face Scrutiny
item.description: Chinese asset managers are reviewing their bond holdings to identify issuers at risk of rating downgrades after regulators stepped up efforts to curb the over-concentration of AAA ratings, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Spain Lifts 2026 Economic-Growth Forecast Despite Iran War
item.description: Spain will continue to outperform the euro area’s largest economies, with the conflict in Iran expected to have only a limited impact, according to new government forecasts.

item.title: Dubai Stocks Set for Best Quarter in a Year as War Premium Ebbs
item.description: Dubai’s stocks index is set for its best quarter in a year after a truce in the Middle East conflict drew investors back to a market they fled when Iranian missile attacks shattered the city-state’s reputation for stability.

item.title: Goldman Backs India 30-Year Bonds as Iran War Impact Contained
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. recommends buying India’s 30-year government bonds as inflation expectations ease and lower oil prices reduce fiscal risks.

item.title: UK Mortgage Approvals Drop as Rush to Lock in Rates Fades
item.description: UK mortgage approvals dropped to the lowest in over two years in May, as a scramble to lock in loans in anticipation of interest-rate hikes fizzled out.

item.title: Godiva Japan Lenders Said to Agree on Nine-Month Loan Extension
item.description: Lenders to Godiva Japan Inc. have agreed to extend by nine months the maturity of about ¥75 billion ($463 million) in loans to the struggling luxury chocolate maker, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Baidu’s CFO on How It Became a Full-Stack AI Player
item.description: From Internet search to chips, LLMs, clouds, and self-driving cars.

item.title: Odd Lots: Baidu’s CFO on Building a Full AI Stack (Podcast)
item.description: Baidu Inc Photographer: Tomohiro Ohsumi/Bloomberg

item.title: ECB’s Kazaks Says No Need for Multiple Hikes in Rushed Way
item.description: The European Central Bank doesn’t need to raise borrowing costs several times quickly, Governing Council member Martins Kazaks told Econostream Media.

item.title: Moody’s Says India Can Handle Modest Fiscal Slippage This Year
item.description: India can withstand a potentially wider-than-forecast fiscal deficit this year without jeopardizing its investment-grade rating, according to Moody’s Ratings, as higher energy prices are expected to pose only temporary budget pressures.

item.title: Korean Stocks Rebound as Samsung, SK Hynix Unveil Spending Plan
item.description: South Korean stocks recouped earlier losses as Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc. pledged massive investment during a government briefing that underscored the country’s commitment to AI development.

item.title: BAT Job Cuts, Outsourcing to Hit 20% of Staff by Year-End
item.description: British American Tobacco Plc is reducing its 47,000-strong global workforce by about one-fifth as part of its sweeping plan to bring down costs and simplify operations.

item.title: Spanish Inflation Unexpectedly Holds Well Above ECB Target
item.description: Spanish inflation unexpectedly remained far above the European Central Bank’s 2% target in the first reading since the US-Iran peace deal that’s brought down energy prices.

item.title: Bridgepoint to Buy Kayne Anderson Real Estate for $1.4 Billion
item.description: UK private equity firm Bridgepoint Group Plc has agreed to buy Florida-based Kayne Anderson Real Estate, the group’s first push into US property.

item.title: Brexit Makes Inflation Spirals More Likely, Says Bank of England Economist
item.description: Britain’s efforts to control inflation are being made harder by the country’s exit from the European Union, the Bank of England’s chief economist warned.

item.title: Lindt Heads for Worst Quarter in 17 Years on Price-Hike Fallout
item.description: Lindt & Spruengli AG’s shares are on track for their biggest quarterly loss in 17 years as consumers tire of higher chocolate prices, signaling that the Swiss confectioner’s strategy of passing on the cost of cocoa may be running out of room.

item.title: Amazon’s Rapid Delivery Push Triggers $15 Billion Rout for Eternal, Swiggy
item.description: Eternal Ltd. and Swiggy Ltd. got the 10-minute delivery party going in India. Now, e-commerce titans Amazon.com Inc. and Walmart Inc.’s Flipkart are looking to crash it.

item.title: Prosus Profit Jumps as Bets on E-Commerce Businesses Pay Off
item.description: Prosus NV said profit more than doubled in the last fiscal year, beating analysts’ estimates as growth in the tech investment company’s e-commerce businesses and an increase in the value of the stake it owns in China’s Tencent Holdings Ltd. boosted earnings.

item.title: FTSE 100 Futures Steady as Oil Stays Around $72
Verizon and BT Create Joint Venture for International Arms
item.description: Verizon Communications Inc. and the UK’s BT Group Plc have agreed to create a joint venture for their international businesses that will have a combined yearly revenue of about $4 billion.

item.title: Pakistan Urgently Seeks LNG as Hormuz Flare-Up Chokes Supply
item.description: Pakistan is seeking to buy liquefied natural gas for delivery this week as a string of attacks in the Strait of Hormuz disrupts flows of the super-chilled fuel.

item.title: China Tech ETF Sees Record Inflow Despite Global Chip Selloff
item.description: Chinese investors poured a record amount into an exchange-traded fund focusing on communication devices on Friday despite a global semiconductor selloff, underscoring resilient demand for the country’s hardware stocks.

item.title: Hormuz Traffic Drops Off as Vessel Attacks Raise Fresh Concerns
item.description: Commercial traffic through the Strait of Hormuz persisted at a reduced level after attacks on two ships raised fresh concerns about the ability and willingness of shipowners to traverse the waterway.

item.title: Samsung, SK Hynix to Build Two Chip Fabs in $518 Billion Project
item.description: The Samsung Electronics Co. semiconductor plant in Pyeongtaek, South Korea. Photographer: SeongJoon Cho/Bloomberg

item.title: Corn Drops After US-Iran Halt Attacks as Traders Weigh US Heat
item.description: Corn futures slipped after hostilities between the US and Iran eased, while traders monitored hot weather in the US that could impact crops.

item.title: German AI Rollout Offers €300 Billion Fix for Worker Shortage
item.description: At a made-to-order homebuilder in northwest Germany, processing more than 250 invoices a week used to swallow the equivalent of four working days. After introducing artificial intelligence last year, the task takes half as long.
